codeReview.summary: The m1-validators feature is a comprehensive and well-implemented Zod validation system for all domain types. The implementation covers Mission, Feature, Task, and Worker validators with complete schema validation, circular dependency detection, and rich error messages. All 118 tests pass, demonstrating excellent test coverage.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill does not have a SKILL.md file in the .factory/skills/ directory, unlike the built-in maestro skills. The worker used mission-worker-base and backend-worker skills during implementation, but no specific skill documentation exists for backend-worker pattern reference.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows skill invocations for 'mission-worker-base' and 'backend-worker', but Glob search for .factory/skills/**/SKILL.md only found built-in maestro skills.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The validator implementation correctly follows the AGENTS.md TypeScript conventions: uses interface for object shapes, type for unions, avoids any, uses readonly for immutable data, and has explicit return types for public functions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/domain/validators.ts: All schemas use strict typing, custom error classes extend Error properly, and type guards use safeParse pattern.

recommendations: Consider adding SKILL.md for backend-worker skill to document patterns for future workers
The circular dependency detection in validateTask() requires allTasksInFeature context - document this dependency requirement in AGENTS.md if not already present
Consider exporting the ASSERTION_ID_PATTERN regex for reuse in other modules that need to validate assertion IDs
summary: m1-validators implementation is complete and high-quality. All requirements fulfilled: Zod validators for Mission, Feature, Task, Worker with schema validation, circular dependency detection using DFS, assertion ID format validation, rich error messages with path tracking, and comprehensive type guards. 118 tests pass. No blocking issues.
